title: cineraria × hinniculus
intro: Cineraria × hinniculus, also known as Mexican Marigold or Buttercup, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, the plant's flowers, leaves, and roots have been used in herbal medicine, particularly in Mexico, to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties. The plant's roots have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting wound healing. Cineraria × hinniculus is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for a variety of preparations to be used for medicinal purposes.
